executable.title: Difference of Gaussians (DOG) Edge Filter
executable.description: Computes DOG(f) = G_sigma1(f) - G_sigma2(f) to produce an edge-enhanced image. The output is rescaled so that the minimum maps to 0 and the maximum maps to 255, and stored as an unsigned char NIFTI image. sigma1 != sigma2; typically sigma2 > sigma1. ECE:5490 HW04 — Partho Ghosh
executable.acknowledgements: Developed as part of ECE:5490 Multi-Dimensional Image Analysis Tools, Spring 2026, University of Iowa. Course instructor: Professor Hans Johnson. This work uses the Insight Toolkit (ITK), an open-source library for medical image analysis developed by the National Library of Medicine (NLM) and the Insight Software Consortium (ISC). Slicer Execution Model (SEM) developed by Kitware Inc. and the Slicer community (slicer.org). For understanding and help with the coding and writing Claude.ai has been used.

parameters.label: Filter Parameters
parameters.description: Sigma values for the two Gaussian filters.

double.name: sigma1
double.description: Sigma for the first (narrower) Gaussian. Must differ from sigma2.
double.label: Sigma 1

double.name: sigma2
double.description: Sigma for the second (wider) Gaussian. Must differ from sigma1. Typically sigma2 > sigma1.
double.label: Sigma 2

image.name: outputVolume
image.label: Output Volume
image.description: DOG edge-enhanced output image (unsigned char, range 0-255).
